description: Manually fixed archetype taxonomy for Stage 1. This replaces VLM-generated taxonomy discovery as the default taxonomy source.

notes: Use this fixed taxonomy as the authoritative archetype set for Stage 1.
VLM may still be used later for class-to-archetype mapping or image-level attribute extraction, but not for discovering the taxonomy itself.
Prefer more specific archetypes over fallback buckets such as household_object.
Places, buildings, and large man-made structures should be grouped under structure_or_building.
Natural landscapes and landforms should be grouped under natural_scene_or_landform.

archetypes.name: animal
archetypes.definition: Animals, animal-like organisms, and extinct/prehistoric animal-form creatures.
includes: insects, arachnids, crustaceans, worms, jellyfish-like invertebrates
mammals including dogs, cats, bears, primates, ungulates, whales

archetypes.name: plant_or_fungus
archetypes.definition: Plants, plant parts, crops, seeds, fruits in botanical form, and fungi/mushrooms handled together for Stage 1.
includes: crops and seeds such as corn, acorn, rapeseed

archetypes.name: food_and_drink
archetypes.definition: Edible or drinkable items, including ingredients, dishes, desserts, and beverages.
includes: prepared foods such as pizza, hotdog, cheeseburger
fruits and vegetables used as food items
drinks such as wine, espresso, eggnog
excludes: containers such as cups, bowls, bottles

archetypes.name: vehicle
archetypes.definition: Transportation vehicles intended primarily for moving people or goods.
includes: military or emergency vehicles such as tank, ambulance, fire engine

archetypes.name: clothing_and_wearable
archetypes.definition: Wearable garments, footwear, headwear, protective wear, and personal fashion accessories.
includes: hats, helmets, ties, bras, bikinis, masks, wigs
wearable protective gear such as knee pads

archetypes.name: furniture
archetypes.definition: Large furnishing objects used for seating, sleeping, storage, or support in home/office environments.

archetypes.name: container
archetypes.definition: Objects primarily designed to hold, store, carry, or pour other items or substances.
excludes: container ship, which should be vehicle

archetypes.name: tool
archetypes.definition: Handheld manual tools or implements used to manipulate, cut, strike, grip, measure, or modify objects.
includes: ladle, spatula, can opener, corkscrew, plane

archetypes.name: device_or_appliance
archetypes.definition: Mechanical, electrical, or electronic devices and appliances with functional operation beyond simple manual tooling.
includes: camera, projector, oscilloscope, radio, television, phone
microwave, refrigerator, dishwasher, washing machine, vacuum cleaner

archetypes.name: weapon
archetypes.definition: Objects primarily designed for combat, attack, defense, or weaponized use.
includes: shield, scabbard, breastplate when treated as combat gear
notes: Warplane and tank should remain under vehicle because their visual prototype is closer to a vehicle archetype.

archetypes.name: sports_or_toy
archetypes.definition: Sports equipment, game objects, hobby items, toys, and play/recreation artifacts.
includes: baseball, basketball, soccer ball, tennis ball, volleyball
teddy bear, jigsaw puzzle, joystick, pinwheel

archetypes.name: household_object
archetypes.definition: Small everyday household objects that are not better grouped as furniture, container, tool, or appliance.
includes: lamp, wall clock, pillow, towel, doormat
soap dispenser, salt shaker, toilet seat, toilet tissue
small domestic objects without a cleaner specialized archetype
notes: Use this as a fallback bucket only after excluding more specific archetypes.

archetypes.name: structure_or_building
archetypes.definition: Buildings, architectural structures, built infrastructure, and place-like man-made structures.
includes: church, mosque, monastery, palace, castle, library, restaurant
barn, greenhouse, bookshop, grocery store, barbershop
bridge, viaduct, breakwater, dam, lighthouse, beacon

archetypes.name: natural_scene_or_landform
archetypes.definition: Natural geographic formations, scenic environments, or landforms.
includes: sandbar, coral reef, geyser, alp, promontory

archetypes.name: human_or_person
archetypes.definition: Human figures, people, or person-role categories.

archetypes.name: text_or_media_object
archetypes.definition: Objects whose primary identity is carrying text, printed information, symbolic signaling, or media content.
notes: traffic light is treated as a symbolic/signaling object here for taxonomy stability.

archetypes.name: decorative_or_symbolic_object
archetypes.definition: Decorative, ceremonial, memorial, religious, or symbolic display-oriented objects.
